title: The Quest for the Lost Talk
subtitle: A PyConDE 2026 Saga
narrator_intro: Tonight, we gather round the scrying pool to consult the audience. Twelve questions. One fate. Let the tale be told.

rounds.poll_question: Pick an ADJECTIVE to describe a heroic PyConista
What's the best Python web framework?

options: I use both and I'm not sorry!

rounds.poll_question: Worst thing to find during a code review?
Most important soft skill for developers?
What do you mass-delete on Fridays?
Pick a PROP for our hero to carry
options: a stress ball shaped like Python

story_template: Once upon a Lightning Talk session at PyConDE 26, a {HERO_ADJECTIVE} PyConista awoke in their hotel room to a horror beyond reckoning: their slides had vanished.

Desperate and determined, they mounted their trusty {MOUNT}, the fastest steed in all the Pythonic Wastes, and set forth on a quest, raising a fist to the sky and bellowing '{BATTLE_CRY}' as their war cry. At the crossroads, Johannes the Cartographer squinted at a crinkled map, pointed vaguely north, and wished them luck.

Deep in the Cave of Legacy Code, guarded by Sebastian the Dark Archivist, they came face to face with a hideous {MONSTER}. The beast hissed and snarled. Our hero raised their staff and cast a devastating spell of {MAGIC_SPELL}, and the monster reeled, but it was not enough.

With a final war-chant of '{WAR_CHANT}', our hero drew their mighty {WEAPON} and charged, felling exactly {ENEMY_COUNT} shadow-minions in a single swing, while Valerio the Chance Caster rolled his enchanted dice and whispered 'determinism is for compilers'.

At the summit of the Mountain of Merge Conflicts, Paula the Chromamancer blessed the path in shades of grey, and our hero unearthed the legendary hoard of {TREASURE}, clutching tight their most prized relic: {RELIC}. But first, at the Bridge of Broken Builds, Alex H. Potter, known across the realm as Alex the Wizard, planted his staff, faced down a shadow of corrupted commits, and thundered 'YOU SHALL NOT PASS… this PR without a code review!'

And when the Final Boss emerged from the Keep of the Dark Archivist, breath reeking of unmerged pull requests, Lisa the Timekeeper raised a glowing hourglass and cried 'you have exactly one lightning talk's worth of time!' Our hero's strategy was clear. They would, quite simply, {FINAL_MOVE}.

It worked. (Somehow.) The slides were recovered. Peace returned to the land of PyConDE. The crowd roared, and our victorious hero celebrated with {VICTORY_DANCE}, while Florian the Wordsmith nodded and whispered: 'the best spells are the ones nobody sees coming.'

fantasy_personas_cameo: Alex H. Potter, a.k.a. Alex the Wizard
